name: Mixed Vegetable Cutlets
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Green peas, frozen or fresh
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cauliflower, potatoes, fresh peas & green beans in the bottom of a pot and cover with water. Put the carrots, beets & celery in a steaming basket, cover and place over the pot. Cook until the vegetables are tender-crisp. Drain, saving the water.
2. Add the frozen peas (defrosted first) to the steamed vegetables.
3. In a blender, process the chilies while running. Add chickpeas, nuts, seeds, and vegetable water if needed. Process until the peas are mashed; some of the liquid may help facilitate this.
4. Add cauliflower, potatoes, fresh peas (if using), and beans, pulsing on and off until coarsely mashed. Transfer to a mixing bowl.
5. Stir in steamed vegetables, salt, pepper, turmeric, garam masala, shredded carrots, beets, celery, flour, soy milk, and poppy seeds; mix well.
6. Divide into portions.
7. Line a baking sheet with waxed paper. Flatten a portion into an almond-shaped cutlet about ½ inch thick and place on the sheet. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
8. Whisk soy milk, cornstarch, and flour in a flat bowl until smooth. Dip each cutlet in batter, roll in poppy seeds briefly, then set aside to air dry.
9. Heat ghee to a depth of about ¾ inch. Fry the cutlets in batches for 2 minutes on each side, draining, then serve.
